meta.resolution_methodology: KB-driven graph navigation with anti-hallucination verification

test_cases.title: Verify User Registration with Valid Data
test_cases.resolution_summary: This test case requires a user registration flow that is NOT part of the existing AURA framework. The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows (landing page → HPID authentication → plan selection → shipping/billing → confirmation → thank you). The test case describes a generic user registration form with username, email, password, confirm password, first name, last name fields, which does not match any existing page object or flow in the framework. Foundation files and base infrastructure are resolved, but all registration-specific components must be created.

relevant_files.reason: Base class for all page objects - provides common element interaction methods that will be used by the new RegistrationPage and SuccessPage objects that need to be created
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - required for any page object

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file for test setup, email generation, and common utilities - needed for generating unique test data (username, email) and test setup operations
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - required foundation file for every test

relevant_files.reason: Browser lifecycle management - required for every test to initialize browser, manage page instances, and handle browser cleanup
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - required foundation file for every test

relevant_files.reason: Configuration management - provides constants, logger, and global state management including base URL configuration
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - required foundation file for every test

relevant_files.reason: General utility functions including random string generation for creating unique usernames and test data
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - utility file for data generation

relevant_files.reason: Pytest configuration and fixtures - provides test setup, teardown, and reporting infrastructure
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - pytest infrastructure file

relevant_files.reason: Test reporting and validation mechanism - used for all assertions and validation steps throughout the test
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - validation and reporting mechanism

relevant_files.reason: Playwright assertions - required for all element state verifications and assertions
relevant_files.kb_verification: Confirmed by KB - required for assertions

relevant_locators_summary.note: The AURA framework does not contain page objects for generic user registration forms. All locators needed for this test case must be created in new page objects.

test_cases.flow_pattern: Navigate to application home page → Click Register button → Fill registration form (username, email, password, confirm password, first name, last name) → Submit form → Verify success message and page navigation. This flow is NOT part of the existing AURA framework which focuses on HP Instant Ink enrollment.

components_to_create.name: HomePage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the application home page containing the Register button and home page container elements. Should inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: The AURA framework has pages/landing_page.py for HP Instant Ink landing page, but no generic home page for user registration applications. This page object must be created.

locators_needed.description: Main container element of home page for page load verification
Register/Sign Up button that navigates to registration page

methods_needed.description: Navigate to application base URL and wait for home page to load
Click the Register button to navigate to registration page
Verify home page is displayed by checking container visibility

components_to_create.name: RegistrationPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the user registration page containing all registration form fields (username, email, password, confirm password, first name, last name) and submit button. Should inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: The AURA framework has pages/hpid_page.py for HP ID authentication, but no generic registration page with username/first name/last name fields. The HPID page is specific to HP ID sign-up (email and password only). This page object must be created for generic user registration.

locators_needed.description: Submit button to submit registration form
Processing indicator shown during form submission
Error message for username field validation
Error message for email field validation
Error message for password field validation
Error message for confirm password field validation
Error message for first name field validation
Error message for last name field validation

methods_needed.description: Verify registration page and form are displayed
Clear and enter username in username field
Clear and enter email in email field
Clear and enter password in password field
Clear and enter confirm password in confirm password field
Clear and enter first name in first name field
Clear and enter last name in last name field
Click submit button to submit registration form
Composite method to fill all registration form fields
Verify processing indicator is shown after form submission
Get the value of a specified form field
Check if error message is visible for specified field

components_to_create.name: SuccessPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the registration success/confirmation page containing success message and success page container. Should inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: The AURA framework has pages/thank_you_page.py for enrollment completion and pages/confirmation_page.py for order review, but these are specific to HP Instant Ink enrollment flows. A generic success page for user registration does not exist and must be created.

locators_needed.description: Success message element displaying registration success confirmation

methods_needed.description: Verify success page is displayed
Verify success message is visible
Get the text content of success message
Verify success message contains expected text

components_to_create.name: RegistrationHelper
components_to_create.description: Helper class to orchestrate the complete user registration flow from home page navigation through form submission to success verification. Should provide high-level methods that use HomePage, RegistrationPage, and SuccessPage objects.
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: The AURA framework has helper/enrollment_helper.py for HP Instant Ink enrollment and helper/hpid_helper.py for HP ID authentication, but no helper for generic user registration flows. This helper must be created to orchestrate the registration workflow.

methods_needed.description: Navigate from home page to registration page by clicking Register button
Complete entire registration flow: fill form and submit
Verify registration completed successfully with expected success message

components_to_create.name: generate_unique_username
components_to_create.description: Function to generate unique username using timestamp or random string to ensure uniqueness across test runs
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: test_flows_common.py has generate_unique_email() but no generate_unique_username() method was confirmed by KB. This function must be created or added.

components_to_create.name: generate_test_password
components_to_create.description: Function to generate valid test password meeting complexity requirements (uppercase, lowercase, number, special character)
components_to_create.reason: KB verification: No password generation utility was confirmed by KB. This function should be created to generate valid test passwords.

components_to_create.name: check_user_exists
components_to_create.description: API helper to check if a user with given username or email already exists in the system
components_to_create.reason: Needed for precondition verification to ensure test data uniqueness. No such API helper exists in the AURA framework.
components_to_create.signature: check_user_exists(username: str = None, email: str = None) -> bool

components_to_create.name: delete_user_account
components_to_create.description: API helper to delete test user account for cleanup after test execution
components_to_create.reason: Needed for test cleanup to remove test users created during test execution. No such API helper exists in the AURA framework.
components_to_create.signature: delete_user_account(username: str = None, email: str = None, user_id: str = None) -> bool

execution_order.action: Generate unique email for test data
Generate unique username for test data
Click Register button to navigate to registration page
Verify registration page and form are displayed
Fill all registration form fields with test data
Verify success message contains expected text

key_findings: The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows, not generic user registration
No page objects exist for generic registration forms with username/first name/last name fields
HPIDPage exists but is specific to HP ID authentication (email and password only)
LandingPage exists but is for HP Instant Ink landing page, not generic home page
ThankYouPage and ConfirmationPage exist but are specific to enrollment completion, not generic registration success
Foundation files (test_flows_common, playwright_manager, settings, base_page_object) are available and confirmed
Email generation utility exists in test_flows_common, but username generation does not
No API helpers exist for user management (check user exists, delete user)
All registration-specific page objects, helpers, and utilities must be created

automation_readiness.readiness_explanation: Only foundation infrastructure (30%) is available. All test-specific components (70%) must be created before automation can begin.
blockers: No page objects exist for the registration flow pages
No helper exists to orchestrate registration workflow
No API helpers exist for user management and cleanup
automation_readiness.estimated_creation_effort: High - requires creating 3 page objects, 1 helper, 2 utilities, and 2 API helpers from scratch

summary.framework_coverage_explanation: The AURA framework provides foundation infrastructure (browser management, test setup, base page objects, utilities) but does not contain any components specific to generic user registration flows. The framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment, not generic user management.
critical_gaps: No page objects for generic user registration forms
No helpers for user registration workflows
recommendations: Create HomePage, RegistrationPage, and SuccessPage page objects inheriting from BasePageObject
Create RegistrationHelper to orchestrate the registration workflow
Add generate_unique_username() utility to test_flows_common.py or core/utils.py
Create user management API helpers for precondition checks and cleanup
Consider whether this test case belongs in the AURA framework or if a separate framework for user management testing should be created
If this is a new application area, establish page object and helper patterns consistent with existing AURA framework conventions
